message:
style(core): add clippy lint suppressions across frankenterm-core modules

Add file-level #![allow(...)] attributes to 16 frankenterm-core source
and test files to suppress noisy clippy warnings that are false positives
or stylistic disagreements in generated/migration code:

- clippy::float_cmp: floating-point comparisons are intentional in SLO
  threshold checks and performance contract validation
- clippy::similar_names: variable naming in these modules follows domain
  terminology where similar names (e.g., warm_bytes/warm_pages) are
  semantically distinct and clear in context
- clippy::overly_complex_bool_expr: complex boolean guards in migration
  rehearsal and cutover logic express multi-condition domain rules that
  are clearer as single expressions
- unused_parens: parentheses used for clarity in compound expressions

Affected modules: asupersync_observability, canary_rehearsal,
context_budget, cutover_evidence, cutover_playbook, migration_rehearsal,
ntm_decommission, resize_scheduler, robot_api_contracts,
robot_sdk_contracts, runtime_performance_contract, runtime_slo_gates,
slo_conformance, ux_scenario_validation, workflows/engine, and
tests/proptest_dependency_eradication.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
